prompt:
Implement a text table formatter in three stages inside src/table.js and re-export everything from src/index.js.

Stage 1 — implement renderTable(rows): rows is a non-empty array of arrays of strings, all rows the same length. Each column is padded with spaces to its widest cell; columns are joined by a single space; the LAST COLUMN IS NEVER PADDED (no trailing spaces on any output line); lines are joined with '\n' (no trailing newline). Example: renderTable([["id","name"],["1","Ada Lovelace"],["42","Bo"]]) must return exactly "id name\n1  Ada Lovelace\n42 Bo".

Stage 2 — extend renderTable(rows, options) with options.align: an array of 'left'|'right' per column. 'right' pads the cell on the left instead of the right. Omitting options entirely or omitting options.align means all columns are 'left', and the output must be BYTE-IDENTICAL to Stage 1 for the same input. A right-aligned last column is still padded on the left to its column width but still produces no trailing spaces. Example: renderTable([["id","qty"],["7","100"],["1234","5"]], {align:["right","right"]}) must return exactly "  id qty\n   7 100\n1234   5".

Stage 3 — extend options further: options.header: true treats the first row as a header and inserts one separator line immediately after it. The separator is '-' repeated to the length of the longest rendered line. Example: renderTable([["id","name"],["1","Bo"]], {header:true}) must return exactly "id name\n-------\n1  Bo". Stage 3 also adds a second exported function renderTableLegacy(rows) that must behave exactly like Stage 1 renderTable(rows) for all inputs, forever. Export both renderTable and renderTableLegacy from src/table.js and from the barrel src/index.js.

After implementing all three stages, every earlier stage's exact output contract must still hold.
